rationale: The citing paper meta-analyzes econometric estimates of personal selling elasticity and notes heavy coverage of pharmaceutical detailing contexts, while the cited paper is an econometric study of physician detailing effects in a U.S. antihistamine category over time. Given this strong topical/method overlap (detailing response, life-cycle dynamics, endogeneity-aware estimation), the citation is plausibly substantive (e.g., an included empirical source or key prior result) rather than an obvious incidental mention.
